name: AI Application Deployer (EU market)
description: Curated framework bundle for an entity that deploys an AI system in the EU — i.e. uses a foundation model or other AI system under its authority for a specific use-case (HR triage, fraud detection, content moderation, customer-support agents).

crosswalk_pairs.rationale: Art. 14 human oversight + Art. 26 deployer obligations map to ISO 42001 Cl. 8.1 system-impact and operational controls — 42001 gives auditable shape to deployer duties.
Art. 9 (deployer-side risk re-assessment) ↔ MAP-1 / MEASURE-1; Art. 14 ↔ GOVERN-2 — NIST AI RMF supports the day-to-day operationalisation of Art. 26.
Inference data and prompt / completion logs fall under Art. 32 — ISO 27001 Annex A.5 / A.8 evidence supports the security posture.

sequencing_notes: 1. Classify the deployment FIRST — is the use-case in Annex III (high-risk)? Is it Art. 5 prohibited? Is it Art. 50 transparency-only (chatbot, deepfake disclosure)? The classification drives the whole obligation set.

2. For high-risk deployments: Art. 14 (human oversight design + competence + intervention authority) and Art. 27 Fundamental-Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) MUST be completed BEFORE first use — Art. 27 must be sent to the national market-surveillance authority before deployment.

3. Art. 26 deployer obligations — use the system in accordance with provider instructions, monitor for serious incidents (report under Art. 73), log automatically for Annex III deployments, inform affected workers (worker-rep notification) and natural persons subject to high-risk AI decisions.
4. GDPR Art. 22 right against solely-automated decisions plus Art. 35 DPIA usually fire together with EU AI Act high-risk classification — combine the DPIA + FRIA into a single workflow.
5. ISO 42001 Cl. 8 (operational) as the standards-based deployer-side QMS; certification helps with public-sector procurement.
6. NIST AI RMF Profiles for the specific use-case (e.g. hiring, lending) as the day-to-day risk-handling reference.

7. Sector overlays — DORA if you're a financial entity, NIS2 if you operate critical infrastructure, HIPAA if you process PHI for US patients (Business-Associate Agreement chain through the model provider).
